name: api-endpoint-creation
description: Next.js 15+ API endpoint creation patterns with Supabase and workspace validation
capabilities: Generate GET and POST API endpoint patterns for Next.js 15+
Implement Supabase server-side authentication and database access
Add workspace validation to ensure multi-tenant data isolation
Use standardized response helpers for consistent API output
Wrap endpoints with error boundary handlers for graceful error management
author: CleanExpo
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill contains standard Next.js API patterns with Supabase integration. HTTP methods (GET/POST), TypeScript code blocks, and standard workspace validation logic were incorrectly flagged by the static scanner. No malicious patterns present.
valueStatement: Building multi-tenant API endpoints requires careful workspace isolation and authentication. This skill provides ready-to-use patterns for Next.js 15+ API routes with Supabase integration and proper workspace validation.
limitations: Requires existing Supabase setup and configured helper utilities
Does not generate authentication logic from scratch
Assumes workspace_id is passed via query parameters
Focused on REST patterns, does not cover GraphQL or WebSocket endpoints

useCases.title: Build Multi-Tenant REST APIs
useCases.description: Create API endpoints that automatically filter data by workspace_id, ensuring each organization only accesses their own data in a shared database.

useCases.title: Standardize API Response Formats
useCases.description: Apply consistent successResponse and errorResponse patterns across all endpoints to create a uniform API interface for frontend consumers.

useCases.title: Secure API Routes with Error Handling
useCases.description: Wrap API handlers with error boundaries to catch exceptions, log errors, and return appropriate HTTP status codes instead of exposing stack traces.

promptTemplates.title: Basic GET Endpoint
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Next.js 15 GET API endpoint in src/app/api/items/route.ts that retrieves items from Supabase filtered by workspace_id from query params.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a simple read-only endpoint to fetch data

promptTemplates.title: POST Endpoint with Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a POST endpoint that validates input fields, creates a new record in Supabase with workspace_id and user ID, and returns the created record with 201 status.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to create new resources through the API

promptTemplates.title: Full CRUD Pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a complete API route with GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE handlers that all validate workspace access before performing database operations.
promptTemplates.scenario: When building a full resource management API

promptTemplates.title: Custom Error Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an API endpoint that handles specific error cases with custom error messages, logs errors for monitoring, and returns appropriate client-friendly error responses.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need detailed error reporting and logging

bestPractices: Always validate workspace_id before any database operations to prevent cross-tenant data access.
Use the withErrorBoundary wrapper on every endpoint to ensure uncaught errors return proper HTTP responses.
Filter ALL Supabase queries by workspace_id without exception to maintain multi-tenant security.
antiPatterns: Skipping workspace validation for internal endpoints - all endpoints need validation.
Hardcoding table names - use dynamic table names from configuration when possible.
Returning raw database errors - always catch errors and return sanitized messages.

faq.question: What workspace_id validation library should I use?
faq.answer: This skill uses the validateUserAndWorkspace helper which should be imported from your project's lib/api-helpers module.

faq.question: Can I use this for DELETE or PUT endpoints?
faq.answer: Yes, the same patterns apply. Create handlers for DELETE, PUT, or PATCH using the same workspace validation and error handling approach.

faq.question: Do I need to install any dependencies?
faq.answer: This skill assumes you already have @supabase/supabase-js installed and have configured getSupabaseServer in your lib/supabase module.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination?
faq.answer: Add pagination parameters to the query and use Supabase's .range() method. The workspace_id filter must still be applied first.

faq.question: Can this work with Edge runtime?
faq.answer: The current pattern uses Node.js runtime features. For Edge runtime, you would need to use @supabase/ssr and adjust the authentication approach.

faq.question: How do I test these endpoints?
faq.answer: Ensure your test environment includes mock workspace validation and Supabase responses. Use the workspace_id from test fixtures to verify isolation.
